ogTitle: Stony Creek vs Stradbroke: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stony Creek and Stradbroke.
heading: Stony Creek vs Stradbroke — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stony Creek, Victoria and Stradbroke, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stony Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Stradbroke (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stony Creek or Stradbroke safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stony Creek has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Stradbroke scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stony Creek scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Stradbroke scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stony Creek or Stradbroke?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stony Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
